title: [phase:critique] [family:policy-review] [provider_hint:codex] Review /Users/masayuki/Dev/tmp/openclaw-tmux-replacement-evidence.md conceptually. Identify the top missing evidence items that still block implementing a bounded policy-gated tmux wrapper. Do not edit files. Return concise bullets priori...
summary_text: - **Critical:** No evidence that policy gates are actually enforceable at runtime (who can approve, how approval is bound to `target + action + payload`, expiry/replay protection, non-bypass path). Without this, “gated” mutating ops are still effectively arbitrary control. - **Critical:** No concrete threat-model evidence for keystroke injection and control-sequence abuse (`send-keys`, control chars, multiline/metachar payloads) across real operator prompts. The outline lists tests, but there are no executed results or failure receipts. - **High:** No full real-world callsite/usage inventory artifact (exact callers, frequency, flags, critical workflows). Replacement risk cannot be bounded until actual dependency surface is proven, not just “known references.” - **High:** No deterministic target-identity evidence (stable session/window/pane resolution under churn, collision, stale IDs). This is the main cross-session misfire risk for bounded wrappers. - **High:** No immutable/tamper-evident audit evidence design (event schema, append-only guarantees, correlation IDs, deny/allow traces). Policy gating is not verifiable without this. - **Medium-High:** No canary/rollback stop-condition evidence (automatic triggers, SLO/error thresholds, max blast radius, operator handoff). “Rollback path” is stated but not operationally proven. - **Medium (governance blocker):** No lane-plan/lane-evidence for required parallel adjudication quorum (Codex + 2 non-Codex lanes, or documented fallback errors). Current evidence package is single-lane and policy-noncompliant for non-trivial rollout decisions.
